method: We implement a blind, API-isolated evaluation protocol for assessing VLM capabilities in CFD flow field plausibility assessment. The protocol proceeds in five stages: (1) CFD Case Generation: OpenFOAM simulations produce correct and intentionally erroneous flow fields across 10 canonical scenarios with 6 error types, yielding 60 total simulations. (2) Visualization Rendering: Flow fields are rendered as standardized 2D contour plots using PyVista and Matplotlib with fixed colormaps, axis labels, and resolution. (3) Prompt Construction: For setup-conditioned evaluation, the image is paired with a problem setup text describing geometry, Reynolds/Rayleigh number, boundary conditions, and solver type. For image-only evaluation, only the image is provided. (4) API-Isolated Evaluation: Images are base64-encoded and sent directly to VLM APIs (Anthropic, Google GenAI, OpenAI) with zero filesystem access. Each evaluation is an independent API call with no shared context between items. Three independent trials are conducted per model to assess consistency. A strict isolation barrier prevents any filesystem contamination. (5) Scoring: VLM verdicts (OK or Anomaly) are compared against expert ground truth to compute accuracy, false positive rate, and false negative rate.
caption: API-isolated evaluation protocol for CFD-VisQA. Flow fields are rendered as standardized contour plots, base64-encoded, and sent via direct API calls with optional setup text. An isolation barrier ensures VLMs have zero filesystem access. Three independent trials per model assess consistency.
